rule: Render points as individual SVG <circle> elements with CSS transitions enabled for d3-transition.
rationale: At low density, maintaining object constancy via SVG DOM elements allows for rich interactivity and smooth transitions, adhering to object-based viewing.

condition: Data Density N < 1,000 (Low Density) AND High Spatial Proximity
rule: Implement d3-delaunay or d3-voronoi overlay to expand the hover target area for individual points.
rationale: Resolves occlusion and targeting issues for sparse but clustered data without altering visual position, facilitating precise selection.

condition: Data Density N < 1,000 (Low Density) AND Network/Hierarchy Data
rule: Apply d3-force simulation with collision detection (d3.forceCollide) to prevent node overlap.
rationale: Displacement (Jittering) is computationally affordable at low N to preserve the visibility of all distinct items.

rule: Switch rendering context from SVG to HTML5 Canvas (d3.path context) and apply globalCompositeOperation = 'multiply'.
rationale: DOM manipulation becomes a bottleneck; Canvas rasterization improves performance while blend modes highlight density hotspots.

rule: Reduce mark radius (r) inversely proportional to N (e.g., r = 30 / Math.sqrt(N)) and set alpha (opacity) < 0.3.
rationale: modifying the appearance of items (size/opacity) reduces the occlusion of overlapping markers without removing data.

condition: Data Density 1,000 < N < 10,000 (Medium Density) AND Categorical Axis
rule: Apply deterministic Jittering (random noise within bin width) to categorical coordinates.
rationale: Displacement reveals density in ordinal/nominal distributions where points would otherwise perfectly overlap.

rule: Implement Geometric Zooming: Render points as pixels initially, transition to detailed glyphs only upon zoom-in (transform k > threshold).
rationale: Adheres to 'Overview first' by abstracting details until the viewable area reduces N to a perceivable limit.

rule: Abandon Voronoi overlays; implement spatial indexing (d3-quadtree) for nearest-neighbor search on mousemove.
rationale: Voronoi computation becomes expensive; Quadtree search remains O(log n) for performant tooltips in canvas environments.

condition: Data Density N > 10,000 (High Density) AND Continuous 2D Data
rule: Aggregate points using d3-hexbin to bin data into hexagonal cells, mapping color intensity to bin length.
rationale: Spatial aggregation reduces visual complexity by representing groups of data rather than individuals, eliminating occlusion.

condition: Data Density N > 10,000 (High Density) AND Topology/Terrain focus
rule: Compute Density Contours using d3-contour (marching squares) instead of plotting individual points.
rationale: Abstracts high-density scatter data into topological regions, visualizing distribution shape rather than item position.

condition: Data Density N > 10,000 (High Density) AND Real-time constraints
rule: Apply Stochastic Sampling: Randomly select a subset (e.g., N=2000) for rendering while maintaining distribution statistical properties.
rationale: Reduces N to rendering limits while preserving the 'Overview' shape; exact points are less relevant than global trends.

condition: Data Density N > 10,000 (High Density) AND High Dimensionality
rule: Initialize Dynamic Query widgets (range sliders) to filter N down to viewable threshold before rendering main view.
rationale: User-controlled filtering reduces the dataset to a manageable size before visualization occurs, preventing cognitive overload.

condition: Data Density N > 10,000 (High Density) AND Multi-view Dashboard
rule: Implement Cross-filtering: Brushing a histogram aggregate filters the active dataset in the main Scatterplot/Map.
rationale: Brushing and Linking allows users to isolate subsets of high-density data across multiple perspectives.

condition: Data Density N > 10,000 (High Density) AND Large Image/Map
rule: Implement Semantic Zooming: Aggregate clusters at zoom level 0, resolving to individual points only at zoom level > 5.
rationale: Changes the visual representation based on available screen space, ensuring density never exceeds perceptual limits.

rule: Bypass D3/Canvas rendering loop; bind data buffers to WebGL (via regl or PixiJS) for GPU acceleration.
rationale: CPU-based rendering (SVG/Canvas) fails at extreme N; GPU parallelization is required to maintain interactive frame rates.

rule: Calculate chart aspect ratio such that the average absolute slope of line segments is ~45 degrees (Banking to 45°).
rationale: Optimizes the perceptibility of trends and local rates of change, reducing bias caused by overly flat or steep lines.

condition: Zoom Scale k < 1 (Overview Level) AND Clustered Data
rule: Render `d3.polygonHull` (Convex Hull) outlines around data clusters; hide individual node elements.
rationale: Reduces visual noise by showing group boundaries and spatial extent rather than individual item details.

condition: Zoom Scale k < 1 (Overview Level) AND Map/Geo Data
rule: Simplify geographic paths using Visvalingam-Whyatt algorithm (via topojson.simplify) to remove sub-pixel details.
rationale: Prevents aliasing artifacts and rendering lag by removing geometry that is too small to be seen at low zoom levels.

rule: Reveal text labels using `d3.quadtree` for collision detection; hide labels that overlap higher-priority nodes.
rationale: Ensures legibility by dynamically filtering annotations based on available screen space as the user zooms in.

condition: Zoom Scale 1x < k < 5x (Intermediate Level) AND Network Graph
rule: Transition links from simple straight lines to curved paths (`d3.geoPath` or Bezier) with increased opacity.
rationale: Reveals structural nuance and edge relationships that are suppressed at overview levels to reduce clutter.

condition: Zoom Scale k > 5 (Detail Level) AND Large Dataset
rule: Implement Tile-based Loading: Trigger `fetch()` for data tiles intersecting the current viewport `d3.zoomIdentity`.
rationale: Lazy loading (Details-on-demand) minimizes memory usage by only retrieving granular data when the user requests a focused view.

rule: Switch from Raster/Canvas rendering to DOM/SVG elements to enable full event listeners (click, hover) on individual items.
rationale: Performance is prioritized at low zoom (Canvas), while interactivity and accessibility are prioritized at high zoom (SVG).

rule: Render a 'Minimap' or Overview+Detail viewport highlighting the current zoom extent rect.
rationale: Mitigates the 'Desert Fog' problem where users lose track of their global position during deep exploration.

rule: Temporarily reduce rendering fidelity (e.g., hide edges/labels) while `d3.event.transform` is changing.
rationale: Maintains high frame rates (60fps) during manipulation, restoring full detail only when the interaction stops (debounce).

rule: Define `zoom.scaleExtent([0.5, 20])` and `zoom.translateExtent` to match data bounding box.
rationale: Prevents users from panning into empty 'white space' or zooming excessively beyond the data's resolution.

condition: Zoom Scale k > 5 (Detail Level) AND Time-Series
rule: Switch x-axis tick formatting from Year/Month (e.g., '%Y') to full Date/Time (e.g., '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M').
rationale: Aligns temporal granularity with the visual scale, providing appropriate precision for the visible time window.

condition: Zoom Scale k > 5 (Detail Level) AND Image Data
rule: Replace placeholder thumbnails/points with high-resolution images loaded via `IntersectionObserver`.
rationale: Manages bandwidth by only loading rich media assets when they are large enough to be perceived by the user.

rule: Maintain spatial position of unselected items but reduce opacity (alpha < 0.1) and saturation (grayscale).
rationale: Preserves context (where the selected items fit in the global distribution) while visually emphasizing the active selection.

condition: Multiple Views Share Same Schema (Identical Axis/Scale)
rule: Implement Shared D3 Dispatcher: `d3.dispatch('brush')` triggers simultaneous updates on all matching scales.
rationale: Synchronization of axes ensures that comparisons across views remain valid and reduces cognitive load.

rule: Create a 'Context' chart (height < 20% of main) with a `d3.brushX` that controls the `x.domain` of the 'Focus' chart.
rationale: Decouples navigation (Context) from analysis (Focus), allowing users to maintain a global overview while inspecting details.

rule: Implement Debouncing (wait 100-300ms) on the `brush` event before triggering the update on linked views.
rationale: Prevents the 'laggy cursor' effect by delaying expensive re-rendering calculations until the user pauses interaction.

rule: On Bar Click: Filter Scatterplot data to match the selected category; animate points entering/exiting via `selection.join()`.
rationale: Explicit filtering (removing points) is clearer than highlighting when the goal is to drill down into a specific category.

rule: When a range is selected in Histogram A, update Histogram B to show both 'Global' (background, gray) and 'Local' (foreground, colored) distributions.
rationale: Allows users to see the selected subset's proportion relative to the whole, revealing correlations between dimensions.

rule: Reset all views to default state (100% opacity) if the brush selection is cleared (clicked outside active area).
rationale: Ensures the visualization returns to the 'Overview' state automatically, preventing users from getting stuck in an empty filter state.

rule: Enable `d3.brush` (2D) to select points; highlight corresponding rows in a linked Table View immediately.
rationale: Bridges the gap between visual patterns and raw data, allowing precise identification of outliers seen in the plot.

rule: On Treemap Node Click: Update Bar Chart to aggregate data for the selected subtree (Drill-down).
rationale: Facilitates exploration of hierarchical structures by using the parent view to constrain the scope of the child view.

rule: Calculate the intersection (AND logic) of all active brushes; dim items that do not satisfy ALL conditions.
rationale: Standard boolean AND logic is the expected behavior for multi-attribute filtering, narrowing the search space.

rule: On Map Pan/Zoom: Filter the Chart to show statistics ONLY for the features currently visible within the map bounds.
rationale: Dynamic spatial filtering ensures the statistical summary always reflects the user's current geographic area of interest.

rule: Use a consistent 'Selection Color' (e.g., bright orange/blue) across all views that contrasts with the base palette.
rationale: Cognitive linking depends on color consistency; changing the highlight color across views breaks the mental model of 'same data'.

rule: Compute `d3.Delaunay.from(data)` and render a transparent Voronoi overlay to handle mouse events.
rationale: Optimizes Fitts's Law by expanding the interactive target area to the maximum available space around the point.

rule: On `mousemove`, use `d3.bisector` to find the nearest data index to `x.invert(mouse[0])` and snap the highlight line.
rationale: Eliminates the need for pixel-perfect hovering by mathematically snapping to the closest valid data point.

rule: Calculate position as `event.pageX - tooltipWidth - padding` (flip to left side).
rationale: Context-sensitive placement ensures the information overlay is never clipped by the browser window edge.

rule: Calculate position as `event.pageY - tooltipHeight - padding` (flip to top side).
rationale: Prevents vertical overflow which would otherwise require scrolling or hide critical details.

rule: Display an indeterminate loading spinner or change CSS cursor to `wait` immediately upon trigger.
rationale: 0.1 seconds is the limit for users feeling that the system is reacting instantaneously; delays beyond this require feedback.

rule: Display a determinate progress bar or modal overlay preventing interaction until completion.
rationale: Delays >1s interrupt the user's flow of thought; explicit progress indicators are required to maintain system trust.

condition: Voronoi Overlay AND Outlier Data Points
rule: Apply a Max Radius Filter: Ignore Voronoi cell hover events if `distance(mouse, point) > threshold` (e.g., 50px).
rationale: Prevents 'Spooky Action at a Distance' where a cursor triggers a highlight for a point far away, confusing the user.

rule: Raise the hovered element to the top of the SVG DOM (`selection.raise()`) and add a contrasting stroke.
rationale: SVG rendering order is defined by DOM order; raising ensures the active element is not occluded by neighbors.

condition: Tooltip contains clickable links or interactive elements
rule: Implement a 'Safe Triangle' or delay logic to allow the mouse to traverse from the data point into the tooltip without it closing.
rationale: Standard hover logic closes the tooltip immediately upon leaving the target; a buffer allows interaction with tooltip content.

rule: Implement a 'Bubble Cursor' logic: dynamically resize the capture area based on the distance to the second-nearest neighbor.
rationale: Dynamically adjusting the target area based on local density improves selection speed in varying density plots.
